moo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monom.
morro: noun. A round hill or point of land.
morrow: noun. The next or following day.
mort: noun. Death; especially, the death of game in hunting.
mot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Barí.
motor: noun. A machine or device that converts other energy forms into mechanical energy, or imparts motion.
mow: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Moi (Congo).
ort: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adivasi Odia.
root: noun. The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body, absorbs and stores water and nutrients, and in
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
rotor: noun. A rotating part of a mechanical device; for example, in an electric motor, generator, alternator, or pump.
row: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Dela-Oenale.
tom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tombulu.
tomorrow: adverb. On the day after the present day.
too: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xicotepec de Juárez Totonac.
tor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Togbo-Vara Banda.
two: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 2.
woo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Manombai.
